\subsection{Comparison of HalogenGroups and PFAS-Atlas on the C\&L Inventory}
\label{subsec:clinventory_comparison}

We applied both HalogenGroups and PFAS-Atlas to the C\&L inventory
(clinventory database, \num{28,328} molecules for HalogenGroups,
\num{28,328} for PFAS-Atlas).  The two classifiers were evaluated on a
common set of \num{28,328} molecules for which both tools successfully
produced a result.

\paragraph{PFAS detection rates.}
HalogenGroups identified a fluorine-containing functional group in
\SI{96.0}{\percent} of the processed molecules, while PFAS-Atlas
returned a PFAS classification in \SI{73.6}{\percent}.
The two classifiers agreed on \SI{76.8}{\percent} of the common
molecules (Cohen's $\kappa = 0.178$).
Of the 28,328 paired molecules, 20,736 were classified as
fluorinated/PFAS by both tools (73.2\%), 6,469 were
detected exclusively by HalogenGroups (22.8\%), 112
exclusively by PFAS-Atlas (0.4\%), and 1,011 were
not classified as fluorinated by either tool (3.6\%).

\paragraph{Timing performance.}
HalogenGroups achieved a median per-molecule classification time of
10.371\,ms (95th percentile: 28.843\,ms), while PFAS-Atlas
required 0.986\,ms at the median (34.443\,ms at the 95th percentile).
The overall timing ratio (HalogenGroups / PFAS-Atlas) was 10.52$\times$,
indicating that
PFAS-Atlas was faster.
Detailed breakdowns by molecular size and halogen composition are provided in
Tables~\ref{tab:clinventory_timing_bracket} and
\ref{tab:clinventory_timing_halogen}.{pfg_para if pfasgroups else ''}
